text: Fats that are used as such at the table or during cooking (vegetable oils, vanaspati, butter and ghee) Excessive use of plant and animal based fats elevation of blood lipids thereby increasing the risk of heart disease and other illnesses. leads to the 45 are termed as “visible” fats. Fats that are present as an integral components of various foods are referred to as “invisible” fat. Fats, in processed and ready to eat foods are known as hidden fats. Cereals contain only 2-3% of invisible fat. However, their contribution to overall fat intake is significant as they contribute to bulk of our Indian diets. The small amounts of invisible fat present in various foods add up to a substantial level in our daily diet (about 15 g in rural population and 30g among urban middle-income and
